settings.heading: A clean escalation path for product, order, and support questions
settings.copy: Use this page as the controlled handoff point for direct support, warranty escalation, or business inquiries.
settings.support_note: Keep the contact page practical: when to contact, which route to use, and what users should prepare before reaching out.

settings.heading: Direct the right question to the right support route
settings.copy: Use these cards for direct support, sales, warranty, or channel escalation paths.

settings.title: Product and setup help
settings.copy: Use this card for direct product support or post-purchase issue handling.

settings.title: Claims and warranty guidance
settings.copy: Use this card for warranty policy, claim routes, or eligibility guidance.

settings.title: Shipping and return questions
settings.copy: Use this card for order support, return windows, and shipping expectations.

settings.heading: Send a message with the details support will need
settings.copy: Use this form for direct support, warranty follow-up, business requests, or order-related escalation when self-service resources are not enough.
settings.side_copy: Include the product name, order reference, setup context, and the issue summary so the next step is clear from the first reply.
settings.success_message: Thanks for reaching out. Your message has been sent.

settings.intro_copy: Use the page body for contact forms, office details, business contact information, or any support expectations.
settings.side_heading: Route customers through self-service when it saves time
settings.side_copy: Use the side card to direct customers to downloads, manuals, compatibility, or FAQ before a direct handoff.

settings.heading: Support pages should still reduce unnecessary escalations
settings.copy: Use the final CTA to redirect into support resources when that path is faster than direct contact.
